images.caption: Figure 1:Verifiable rewards beyond math and code.Math and code tasks enjoy programmatic, deterministic reward signals (calculators, compilers). Prior sentence-level factuality methods rely on neural verification pipelines which become costly at RL scale. CorVer fills this gap with a corpus-indexed co-occurrence statistic that requires no neural verifier in the reward loop.

Figure 2:CorVer pipeline.Each sentence is scored for Wikipedia co-occurrence via an Infini-gram index. The per-sentence reward is mapped to token-level returns throughσ\sigmaand combined with response-level signals in a policy-gradient update (instantiated with GRPO in our experiments).
Figure 3:Cross-model scaling of CorVer gains over Rawacross six models (33B to1414B) and five benchmarks. Every cell is positive; full accuracy tables in AppendixC.2.
Figure 4:Average training time per method (hours) across four base models. Bars annotate each baseline’s slowdown relative to CorVer. Detailed breakdowns in AppendixC.4.

Figure 5:EmpiricalP​(correct∣ci)P(\mathrm{correct}\mid c_{i})across five co-occurrence buckets (N=700N=700manually annotated sentences). Green dashed: reward-mapping boundaries in Eq. (2); grey dotted: unused split atci=10c_{i}=10. Error bars are Wilson95%95\%CIs; per-bucket counts in Table13(AppendixB.5).

header: Appendix BCo-occurrence Reward Design and Validation

images.caption: Figure 6:Sensitivity of CorVer accuracy on Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct to the zero-count co-occurrence penaltyric​(ci=0)r_{i}^{\mathrm{c}}(c_{i}=0). Each point is a separate retraining of the full CorVer recipe with only the zero-count penalty changed, evaluated on the TriviaQA validation set (N=17,944N=17{,}944). The vertical axis is the change in correct rate relative to the canonical−0.3-0.3setting (green star).

Figure 8:TriviaQA accuracy of Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct CorVer as a function of GRPO step, evaluated every5050steps up to step300300. The first100100steps account for the largest single jump (+6.85+6.85pp over Raw); step200200is the empirical peak (+8.46+8.46pp); steps250250–300300plateau and drift slightly down. The paper uses the step-100100checkpoint (green dashed line) as the uniform end-of-run selection; step200200(orange dashed line) is the empirical peak when compute is not constrained.
